name: The Kerry Mountain Cattle (foundation stock)
aliases: Tipperary beef down to the heels
bloodline_detail: The small black mountain cattle of south-western Ireland (Kerry, Tipperary) from which the Dexter was selected.
notable: The Dexter descends from the ancient Celtic mountain cattle of Ireland and was, until the mid-1800s, regarded as a short type within the Kerry breed. 'Mr Dexter', agent to Lord Hawarden at the Dundrum House estate in Co. Tipperary, is credited (c.1750) with fixing the type 'by selection of the best of the mountain cattle of the district.'
notes: Origin well-corroborated across ADCA history, Wikipedia, and Killenure Castle tradition; the c.1750 'Mr Dexter' date is traditional, and no individual foundation animal is named in the record.

name: Elmendorf Farm herd (foundation import group)
notable: One of the three principal early US estate herds that received a large share of the 200+ Dexters and Kerries imported to America between 1905 and 1915. Berryman was a founding vice-president of the American Kerry and Dexter Cattle Club (1911).
notes: Named on the ADCA history page. Individual animal names/registration numbers from these estate herds are not exposed on any public page.

name: North Oaks herd (Mrs James J. Hill)
bloodline_detail: North Oaks, Minnesota; established 1914 by Mrs James J. Hill.
notable: A foundation US estate herd and a source of stock behind later American herds. Part of the first great wave of US Dexter importation.
notes: Named on the ADCA history page; no named individual foundation animals publicly listed.

name: Warrior of Hempstead House
bloodline_detail: From Daniel Guggenheim's Hempstead House herd (ex-Castlegould), Long Island, New York.
notable: An early US foundation sire acquired from the Guggenheim herd into the Peerless Herd (Decorah, Iowa) — the oldest continuously-registered US Dexter herd, founded by John Logsdon in 1918.
notes: Named on the ADCA history page; no public registration number found — reg# unverified.

name: Captain of Hempstead House
bloodline_detail: From Daniel Guggenheim's Hempstead House herd, Long Island, New York.
notable: Early US foundation sire acquired alongside Warrior from the Guggenheim herd into the Logsdon family's Peerless Herd.
notes: Named on the ADCA history page; no public registration number found — reg# unverified.

name: Parndon Bullfinch
bloodline_detail: UK-bred, from the Parndon herd; imported to the United States.
importer: Imported from the UK into the US
notable: The first Dexter bull in the United States collected for artificial insemination — his semen was released to breeders in 1968, the origin point of the US Dexter AI program.
notes: Named on the ADCA history page; no public registration number found — reg# unverified. Role (first US AI Dexter, 1968) is well documented.

name: Saltaire Platinum
bloodline_detail: UK-bred polled bull; sire Hill Poldark, dam Saltaire Padrilla, with multiple Woodmagic ancestors.
importer: Semen imported to the US (Fred Chesterley / Llanfair herd)
notable: UK-bred polled bull whose semen was imported to the US in 1994; credited as the fountainhead of essentially all registered polled Dexters in America, and for improving back-end muscling, udder attachment, and docility.
notes: Reg# 6504 and birth year 1990 come from the breeder-run herdbook site dexters4u.com, NOT the official society register — treat as indicative/medium confidence.

name: Whitegates Storm
bloodline_detail: British bull; sire of the first modern in-calf females imported to Australia.
notable: Sired the two in-calf females imported to Australia by Bridie Prettejohn in 1988 — a taproot of the modern Australian Dexter population.
notes: Named on the Dexter Cattle Australia history page; no public registration number found — reg# unverified.

name: Saltaire Guiness
bloodline_detail: Young UK bull imported to Australia in the 1988 foundation import.
notable: Imported to Australia in 1988 alongside the first modern in-calf females; a foundation sire of the Australian breed base.
notes: Named on the Dexter Cattle Australia history page; no public registration number found — reg# unverified.

name: Ballard Family Titan
notable: 2024 ADCA National Expo Grand Champion Bull; current AI sire (semen ~$55/straw).
notes: ADCA reg# verified on the public ADCA AI Dexter Bulls index; 2024 National Expo Grand Champion confirmed on the breeder's own site.

name: Timberview Pepper Jack
notable: Multiple-time state-fair Grand Champion (Nebraska and Utah, 2022) and a sought-after current AI sire (~$55-60/straw).
notes: ADCA reg# 051557 verified on the public ADCA AI Bulls index. Championship claim corrected per fact-check: public sources confirm 2022 Nebraska & Utah STATE Fair Grand Championships (an earlier draft's 'National 2023' title could not be verified).

name: Belle Fourche Rousseau
bloodline_detail: Belle Fourche line; homozygous polled, A2/A2 red.
notable: Highly sought homozygous-polled, A2/A2 AI genetics — one of the premium-priced Dexter sires (~$90/straw).
notes: ADCA reg# verified on the public ADCA AI Bulls index; also on Songwood Haven Farm's semen catalog.

name: CJ's Archie
bloodline_detail: Sire of the seven-time Grand Champion High Pines Ferley.
notable: 2018 ADCA National Grand Champion Bull and an influential modern AI sire (~$75/straw).
notes: ADCA reg# verified on the public ADCA AI Bulls index; 2018 National title corroborated.

name: High Pines Ferley
notable: A seven-time Grand Champion and current AI sire (~$50/straw).
notes: ADCA reg# verified on the public ADCA AI Bulls index; seven-time Grand Champion corroborated.

name: DB Olaf
bloodline_detail: Horned dun; tested non-chondro and non-PHA.
notable: A widely-used current AI sire (~$50/straw), valued as a horned dun that is free of both chondrodysplasia and PHA.
notes: ADCA reg# 051148 verified on the public ADCA AI Bulls index. A championship-title claim (2022 Grand Champion) could not be confirmed on a public page and is omitted.

name: FF Freedom's Harvest Moon
notable: A homozygous-polled, A2/A2 AI sire (~$55-60/straw) — the trait profile most in demand among modern homestead breeders.
notes: ADCA reg# verified on the public ADCA AI Bulls index.

name: Hillview Red Wing
notable: A homozygous-polled red AI sire (~$65/straw) — reliably throws polled, red calves.
notes: ADCA reg# verified on the public ADCA AI Bulls index.

name: September Ranch Oak
notable: A homozygous-dun, A2/A2 AI sire (~$75/straw) prized by breeders selecting for the dun coat.
notes: ADCA reg# verified on the public ADCA AI Bulls index.

name: SMD Brigos Ceti
bloodline_detail: A2/A2; noted for consistent low birth weights.
notable: A calving-ease AI sire (~$65/straw), A2/A2, known for consistently low birth weights over heifers.
notes: ADCA reg# verified on the public ADCA AI Bulls index.
